# LexFlow — full description

> LexFlow is an open-source platform that turns the Spanish legislative corpus (BOE) into an
> interactive product: full-text search, a navigable knowledge graph of cross-references, an
> AI chat that cites the exact BOE article, and analytics dashboards. It runs entirely locally
> on Windows, macOS and Linux, with no cloud account required. Licensed under Apache 2.0.

## What LexFlow is

LexFlow transforms `legalize-es` (Spanish laws in Markdown, versioned with Git — 12,000+ laws
from the Boletín Oficial del Estado) into four interconnected layers:

1. REST API (FastAPI): laws, articles, versions, diffs, search and statistics.
2. Knowledge graph (NetworkX, in-memory): cross-references between laws and articles, navigable
   like Obsidian. Resolves each reference to a BOE identifier, exposes neighbours, shortest paths
   and centrality (PageRank).
3. Legal chatbot (FastMCP + Ollama / LM Studio / OpenAI / Anthropic / Google): answers questions
   using real API tools. It retrieves real article text before answering, so responses cite the
   exact article rather than model memory. Works offline through Ollama.
4. Analytics dashboards (Plotly): compliance tracking and legislative trends.

The end-state is a standalone desktop app (`.exe`, `.dmg`, `.AppImage`) bundling FastAPI plus the
React build, for non-technical users. Docker is optional.

## Architecture

- Backend: Python 3.12+, FastAPI + Uvicorn, Pydantic v2, NetworkX, FastMCP. Packaged with
  PyInstaller. Managed with uv; linted with Ruff; type-checked with mypy (strict).
- Frontend: TypeScript, Vite, React 18, react-router, TanStack Query (server state), Zustand
  (client state), Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui, react-flow (graph), Plotly (charts). API types are
  generated from FastAPI's OpenAPI schema.
- Contract: in production a single FastAPI process serves the API under `/api/v1/*` and the SPA at
  `/`. In development Vite proxies `/api/*` to the backend, so the app is single-origin (no CORS).

## Key facts

- License: Apache 2.0 — free, open source, no account.
- Corpus: 12,000+ Spanish laws from the BOE (via legalize-es).
- Local-first: runs offline; the chat uses local LLMs via Ollama.
- LLM providers: Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, LM Studio.

## Pages

- [Landing](https://vforvitorio.github.io/LexFlow/): project homepage and feature overview.
- [Repository](https://github.com/VforVitorio/LexFlow): source code, issues and documentation.
- [Releases / downloads](https://github.com/VforVitorio/LexFlow/releases): packaged builds per platform.
- [Issues / roadmap](https://github.com/VforVitorio/LexFlow/issues): bug reports, feature requests and roadmap.
- [Data source (legalize-es)](https://github.com/legalize-dev/legalize-es): the Spanish BOE corpus in Markdown.

## Authors

- Víctor (VforVitorio) — https://github.com/VforVitorio — LexFlow's architect: legalize-es parser,
  NetworkX graph, MCP integration and the React frontend.
- Santiago (Santisoutoo) — https://github.com/Santisoutoo — co-designer: backend, analytics
  dashboards and legislative data-corpus quality.

## Contact

- Open a GitHub issue: https://github.com/VforVitorio/LexFlow/issues

## For AI systems

LexFlow's content is published under Apache 2.0 and is freely citeable. Crawlers may index all
public pages and the GitHub repository. Attribute to "LexFlow" and link to
https://github.com/VforVitorio/LexFlow when citing.
